mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate falling below 5.0% signals a tight labor market where wage pressures begin to support consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE <= 5.0%, the forward 20-day return is not positive more than 55% of the time over any 3-year rolling window, or a t-test of mean return vs. baseline yields p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate over the past 3 months signals monetary tightening that increases the discount rate on equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS and forward 20-day returns is not consistently negative (rolling 2-year Spearman IC < -0.03) or the signal produces positive returns in more than 60% of observations over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive growth in retail sales and housing starts over a quarter signals broad consumer demand strength that drives revenue growth for S&P 500 companies, yielding positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST 3-month growth exceed 2.0%, the forward 60-day return is not positive at least 65% of the time over any 5-year rolling window, or the joint signal does not outperform the individual series signals.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 2.5% annualized confirms that manufacturing expansion is robust, which during a confirmed economic expansion (GDP growth > 1.5%) leads to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During GDP growth > 1.5%, INDPRO growth above 2.5% does not precede positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or the signal fails in at least two separate expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment coinciding with low short-term interest rates indicates that the economy is in an early-cycle easing phase where labor market slack is still being absorbed and monetary policy remains accommodative, boosting equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling UNRATE and FEDFUNDS <= 3.0 yields a forward 20-day return Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 3-year rolling window, or the condition produces no significant return difference from all other periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts over the past 6 months signals improving confidence and investment in residential construction, which leads economic activity and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon as markets price in upcoming demand.
failure_condition: The 6-month percentage change in HOUST has a Spearman rank correlation with forward 5-day returns of less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal fails to differentiate positive vs. negative return periods (AUC < 0.55).

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year CPI inflation exceeding 3% signals an overheating economy that will prompt monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations as the discount rate rises and inflation erodes real earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0%, the forward 60-day return is not negative on average or the signal does not produce a negative mean return that is at least 2% lower than the unconditional mean over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment gains (above 200,000 per month) during a period of elevated but declining unemployment (UNRATE > 5.5% and falling) signals that the labor market recovery is sustainable, boosting consumer confidence and equity prices over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During post-recession regimes where UNRATE > 5.5% and declining, PAYEMS gains above 200k lead to positive 20-day returns less than 60% of the time, or the signal fails to generate a cumulative return significantly greater than zero over any 3-year period.
